choice: Two separate Railway services from the same monorepo: a `web` service (Next.js) and a `worker` service (the pg-boss consumer), sharing the one Postgres.
rationale: A long Monte Carlo run (10k paths × 40 years) or a daily equity-value sync shouldn't compete with web request handling or be killed by a web redeploy. Separate services let the worker restart/scale independently and keep the Next.js serverless-ish runtime free of long-lived background loops. Cost is one extra Railway service, which is negligible for a closed tool. This is consistent with the monorepo-with-engine-package and the job-status-panel decisions already made.
